content:
Hi! I am a senior student at KAIST co-majoring in Computer Science and Bio & Brain Engineering.
Advised by professor Seunghoon Hong, I am studying computer vision and artificial intelligence. I am particularly interested in learning visual cognition with minimal supervision, and learning to reason about objects and their interactions in a scene.
Before joining him, I carried out a computational neuroscience research as a co-first author under supervision of professor Se-Bum Paik. Our work addressed development of early visual circuits in absence of experience, and is now published as a cover article in The Journal of Neuroscience!
Apart from work, I enjoy listening to and making music! My favorite musicians include Radiohead, Weyes Blood, Damien Rice, and Ryuichi Sakamoto. My friends and I have an independent composer group named Regular Daydream Club (정기적 멍때리기 클럽), collaborating with webcomic artist Ju Hwan Lee.

title: Jinwoo Kim
Spontaneous Retinal Waves Can Generate Long-Range Horizontal Connectivity in Visual Cortex
Emergence of Global Cortical Activity from Local Interactions
